content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom is always smiling and laughing — he is the most ___ person in our class.
distractor_rationale: cruel means unkind and hurtful, which contradicts smiling and laughing
careless means not paying attention to mistakes, unrelated to smiling or happiness
curious means wanting to learn or know things, not connected to always smiling

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She ran into the burning building to save the child — everyone agreed she was very ___.
distractor_rationale: clever means intelligent, but running into danger to save someone describes courage, not intelligence
fair means treating people equally, which has no connection to entering a burning building
cool means calm or fashionable, neither of which describes the act of risking one's life to save someone

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The boy spilled his drink because he was not ___ enough when he picked up the glass.
distractor_rationale: confident means feeling sure of yourself, which does not explain why something was spilled by accident
ambitious means wanting to achieve great things, unrelated to handling objects with attention
creative means having new ideas, which has no connection to spilling a drink through lack of attention

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria always asks questions about everything around her — her teacher says she is naturally ___ and loves to discover new things.
distractor_rationale: cheerful describes someone who is happy and positive, not someone who constantly asks questions to learn
careless describes someone who makes mistakes through lack of attention, the opposite of someone who actively seeks knowledge
confident describes someone who feels sure of themselves, but asking many questions is specifically a sign of wanting to know more, not of self-assurance

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: David spoke clearly in front of the whole school without feeling nervous — he seemed very ___ on stage.
distractor_rationale: familiar means knowing something well, but it does not describe the feeling of not being nervous while speaking in public
creative means having original ideas, which is unrelated to speaking without nervousness in front of an audience
brave describes courage in dangerous situations, whereas speaking without nervousness in public is specifically described by confidence, not bravery

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The judge listened carefully to both sides and made a decision that treated everyone equally — her colleagues described her as completely ___.
distractor_rationale: clever means intelligent, but treating both sides equally and making balanced decisions specifically describes fairness, not intelligence
ambitious means having strong goals for personal success, which contradicts the idea of impartially treating everyone equally
cheerful means happy and positive in manner, which has no connection to making equal and balanced decisions

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna designed a completely new way to organise the office space using only recycled materials — her manager said she was very ___.
distractor_rationale: careful means paying close attention to avoid mistakes, but inventing a completely new design from unusual materials describes creativity, not carefulness
cool means calm or fashionable, neither of which describes inventing an original solution using recycled materials
familiar means knowing something well already, which contradicts designing something completely new

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Despite working in a junior position, Sara set herself the goal of becoming head of the department within three years — her colleagues admired how ___ she was.
distractor_rationale: confident describes feeling sure of oneself, but setting a high career goal specifically within a fixed timeframe describes ambition, not confidence
clever describes intelligence, but having a strong drive to reach a high professional goal is a quality of ambition, not cleverness
curious describes a desire to learn and ask questions, which does not explain the motivation to set and pursue a specific career target

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The manager often forgot to check his reports for errors and regularly missed important details, which made his colleagues frustrated with his ___ attitude toward his work.
distractor_rationale: cruel means deliberately causing pain or suffering to others, which does not describe forgetting to check reports or missing details
cool means calm and relaxed, but the sentence specifies forgetting to check for errors and missing details, which describes carelessness, not a relaxed personality
fair means treating people equally and justly, which has no connection to making repeated mistakes by failing to check one's own work

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Although Mark had never visited the city before, the streets and buildings felt strangely ___ to him because he had studied so many photographs of the area.
distractor_rationale: attractive means pleasing to look at, but feeling as though you have seen something before because of prior study describes familiarity, not attractiveness
fancy means elaborate or high quality, which does not describe the feeling of recognising a place you have never physically visited
creative means having original ideas, which has no connection to recognising streets and buildings from photographs studied in advance

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The little boy ran into the burning building to save his dog — everyone said he was very ___.
distractor_rationale: clever describes intelligence, not the willingness to face physical danger
cheerful describes a happy mood, which contradicts the dangerous and serious situation described
careful means avoiding risk, which is the opposite of running into a burning building

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria always smiles and laughs with her friends, so everyone finds her very ___ to be around.
distractor_rationale: cruel means unkind and causing pain, which directly contradicts smiling and laughing with friends
curious describes a desire to learn or know things, not a consistently happy and friendly manner
confident describes self-assurance, but the sentence specifically focuses on her happy, smiling nature rather than any belief in her own abilities

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James checked every number in the report three times before sending it, because he is extremely ___ about his work.
distractor_rationale: ambitious describes a strong desire for success or achievement, not the act of repeatedly checking details for accuracy
creative describes the ability to produce new ideas or things, which is unrelated to the act of checking numbers for mistakes
fair describes treating people equally and justly, which has no connection to checking a report multiple times

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The new manager always gives the same rules to every member of the team and never treats anyone differently, so the staff think she is very ___.
distractor_rationale: confident describes self-assurance in one's own abilities, not the equal treatment of others
ambitious describes a strong desire to achieve personal goals, which does not explain applying the same rules to everyone
familiar describes knowing something or someone well, which has no connection to applying equal rules to all team members

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Even though Sara had never spoken in front of a large audience before, she walked onto the stage and delivered her speech without hesitating once, which impressed everyone who had expected her to be nervous.
distractor_rationale: creative describes the ability to produce original ideas, which does not explain the absence of hesitation or nervousness when speaking in public
careless describes a lack of attention to detail and implies mistakes, which contradicts the impression of someone who performed well enough to impress the audience
curious describes an eagerness to learn or explore, which does not account for the composure and lack of hesitation described in the sentence

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The little girl smiled and laughed all day — she was very ___.
distractor_rationale: cruel means unkind and causing pain, which contradicts smiling and laughing all day
careless means not paying attention to mistakes, which is unrelated to smiling and laughing
curious means wanting to know things, which does not describe someone who smiles and laughs all day

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom ran into the burning building to save the child — everyone said he was very ___.
distractor_rationale: clever means intelligent, which does not describe the act of running into danger to save someone
fair means treating people equally, which is unrelated to entering a burning building
cool means calm or fashionable, which does not capture the courageous act described

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The teacher always gave the same marks to every student for the same work — she was completely ___.
distractor_rationale: creative means having new ideas, which does not describe giving equal marks for equal work
confident means sure of oneself, which is unrelated to treating students equally
ambitious means wanting to achieve a lot, which does not describe equal treatment of students

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria always checks her work twice and never makes mistakes — her colleagues say she is very ___.
distractor_rationale: careless means the opposite — not paying attention and making mistakes, which directly contradicts checking work twice
curious means wanting to learn new things, which does not describe someone who checks their work for mistakes
cheerful means happy and smiling, which is unrelated to checking work carefully for errors

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James solved the maths problem in his head in seconds, without using a calculator — he is extremely ___.
distractor_rationale: brave describes courage in dangerous situations, not the ability to solve maths problems mentally
fair describes equal treatment of people, which is unrelated to solving maths problems quickly
attractive describes physical appearance, not the ability to perform mental calculations

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna always speaks in front of large groups without feeling nervous — she is a very ___ presenter.
distractor_rationale: familiar means knowing something well, but does not describe someone who speaks without nervousness
creative means producing new ideas, which does not describe the absence of nervousness when speaking publicly
cruel means causing pain to others, which contradicts the neutral, professional context described

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The young designer came up with ten completely new ideas for the project in one afternoon — she is very ___.
distractor_rationale: careful means checking for mistakes, which does not explain producing ten completely new ideas
confident means not feeling nervous, which is unrelated to generating original ideas
familiar means knowing something well already, which contradicts producing new and original ideas

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Despite working a full-time job, David studied every evening because he was ___ enough to want to reach the top of his profession.
distractor_rationale: curious means wanting to learn about new things, but does not explain the drive to reach the top of a profession through sacrifice
cheerful means happy and positive, which does not explain why someone studies every evening after work
cool means calm or fashionable, which does not describe the motivation to work hard and advance professionally

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The manager punished some employees for the same mistake she ignored in others, so her team felt she was being completely ___.
distractor_rationale: careless means not paying attention, but the manager clearly noticed the mistake — she chose to treat people differently
cruel means causing deliberate suffering, which is too extreme for inconsistent rule-application in a workplace context
curious means wanting to find out about things, which has no connection to inconsistent treatment of employees

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara kept asking her teacher questions about every topic they studied, even after class — she was naturally very ___ about the world around her.
distractor_rationale: ambitious means wanting to achieve success or status, which does not explain asking extra questions about every topic out of interest
confident means feeling sure of oneself, which contradicts the behaviour of someone who keeps asking questions because they want to know more
brave means showing courage in dangerous situations, which is entirely unrelated to asking questions after class

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom is very ___ and always helps his friends. (CARE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She is always smiling and has a very ___ personality. (CHEER)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The young student showed great ___ when she asked the teacher many questions about the topic. (CURIOUS)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: His ___ in front of the large audience surprised everyone, because he had never spoken in public before. (CONFIDENT)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Her colleagues were impressed by the ___ of her designs, which combined ideas from art and science in a way nobody had tried before. (CREATE)
